title: NGE EoE Re release re issue Not impossible
display_title: NGE EoE Re release re issue Not impossible
wikitext:
[]: NGE/EoE Re-release/re-issue? Not impossible             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Jul 20, 2013 1:13 pm Went to Manchester Comic-Con - asked a guy from Manga UK (who did a panel there) about if there was any possibility of re-releasing/re-issuing the original series and/or EoE. He didn't say it wouldn't happen but he did say if it did it would be after the Rebuild films are done with. I think Gainax/Khara are holding out on doing anything with NGE until then. Which is what you'd expect. So thats a glimmer of hope that something might come of it anyway. Also, he hadn't seen 3.0. Heh. The same guy at the panel, when someone asked 'What about the Live Action Eva Movie?', said 'There already is one, it's called Pacific Rim'. Oooh whyioughta... Also encountered the first Rei cosplayer I've ever spoke to. ...

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sun Jul 21, 2013 12:23 pm Seeing as Gainax has been releasing BD of old series, I am hoping they do the same for NGE. With 20th Anniversary coming up and everything, I think we can expect it on BD in near future. Though I do to wonder if they can really push the quality anymore than they have with the Renewal to take advantage of BD. Either way, any possible release outside of Japan will be discussed after the release, so I doubt Manga UK has any real inside information at this point in time. If re-release happen, I'll put my money on 2015, being meaningful year for the Evangelion franchise and all.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 22, 2013 9:27 am I don't know how they could make a BD release with this issue , but maybe it's possible? Personally I'm quite happy with my Platinum/Renewal DVDs. If we want Eva in HD and on Blu-ray, that's what NME is for.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jul 22, 2013 9:42 am Everything benefits from a proper HD remaster. Even low budget TV anime hurridly shot on crappy 16mm film. Film scan technology not only improved since Renewal but more importantly became more available and cheaper. There really is no excuse for them not to rescan everything for the inevitable BD release. The old remaster even if it says was done in HD is guaranteed to be inadequate for a proper BD release simply because of the time when it was done. It will happen. Just pray they don't screw it up with laziness or foolish beliefs that digital post-processing is magic that can fix everything.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:16 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Everything benefits from a proper HD remaster. Even low budget TV anime hurridly shot on crappy 16mm film. Film scan technology not only improved since Renewal but more importantly became more available and cheaper.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:31 pm Yes but keep reading. They have the internegative for ep 16 and that will still do fine.
